diagrams.title: Triple Bottom Line Framework
diagrams.description: Shows the three pillars of sustainability: People (Social), Planet (Environmental), and Profit (Economic)
elements: People - Social equity, health, education, community
Planet - Natural resources, climate, biodiversity, pollution control
Profit - Economic growth, employment, innovation, financial returns
diagrams.purpose: Understanding the interconnected pillars of sustainable development and how they balance each other

diagrams.title: Life Cycle Assessment Stages
diagrams.description: Shows the four stages of product life cycle from cradle to grave
elements: Stage 1: Raw Material Extraction - Mining, agriculture
Stage 3: Use Phase - Consumption, operation, maintenance
Stage 4: End of Life - Disposal, recycling, reuse
diagrams.purpose: Visualizing environmental impact at each stage to identify improvement opportunities

diagrams.title: Linear vs Circular Economy
diagrams.description: Compares the traditional linear model with the sustainable circular model
elements: Circular: Reduce → Reuse → Recycle → Regenerate → Continuous loop
diagrams.purpose: Understanding how circular economy minimizes waste and maximizes resource utilization

diagrams.title: Data Center Energy Flow
diagrams.description: Shows how energy flows in a data center and where PUE is measured
elements: Total Facility Energy = IT Equipment + Cooling + Lighting + Losses
PUE = Total Facility Energy / IT Equipment Energy
diagrams.purpose: Understanding energy efficiency metrics in IT infrastructure
